name: La Ruta de los Olivos Milenarios — Circular desde Xert hasta Canet Lo Roig
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Xert navigate a landscape characterized by varied terrain, including olive groves and mountainous areas. The region features a mix of elevations, from gentle slopes suitable for leisurely rides to more challenging climbs. This diverse topography provides a range of mountain biking experiences. The area offers routes that traverse both open countryside and more secluded paths. Best mountain bike trails around Xert The most popular mountain bike trail is La Ruta de los Olivos Milenarios — Circular desde Xert hasta Canet Lo Roig, a 14.9 miles (23.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 58 minutes to complete. This easy route features ancient olive trees and gentle ascents. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Vallibona and El Turmell Loop, a difficult 28.7 miles (46.2 km) path. It offers significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain, providing panoramic views. Local mountain bikers also love the Bovalar area junction – EL BOVALAR of La Salzadella loop from Sant Mateu, a 31.9 miles (51.3 km) trail leading through the Bovalar area, often completed in about 4 hours 43 minutes. Mountain biking around Xert is defined by olive groves, varied elevations, and open countryside. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to difficult mountain bike trails. The routes in Xert are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Xert's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Xert?
A: There are over 80 mountain bike trails around Xert, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What is the general terrain like for mountain biking in Xert?
A: The terrain around Xert is varied, characterized by olive groves and mountainous areas. You'll find a mix of elevations, from gentle slopes ideal for relaxed rides to more challenging climbs, traversing both open countryside and secluded paths.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Xert?
A: Yes, Xert offers trails suitable for beginners. For an easy ride, consider &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e932015994/la-ruta-de-los-olivos-milenarios-circular-desde-xert-hasta-canet-lo-roig" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Ruta de los Olivos Milenarios — Circular desde Xert hasta Canet Lo Roig&lt;/a&gt;, which is 14.9 miles (23.9 km) long and features ancient olive trees and gentle ascents.

Q: What kind of challenging mountain bike trails can I find in Xert?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Xert has several difficult trails. One option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31469771" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bovalar area junction – EL BOVALAR of La Salzadella loop from Sant Mateu&lt;/a&gt;, a 31.9 miles (51.3 km) route through the Bovalar area. Another demanding trail is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38221505" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mola de Xert – Mas d'en Coll loop from Sant Mateu&lt;/a&gt;, covering 53 miles (85.1 km) with significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Xert?
A: Yes, many routes around Xert are circular. An example of a circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e932015994/la-ruta-de-los-olivos-milenarios-circular-desde-xert-hasta-canet-lo-roig" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Ruta de los Olivos Milenarios — Circular desde Xert hasta Canet Lo Roig&lt;/a&gt;, which allows you to start and end in the same location.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Xert?
A: The mountain bike trails in Xert are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. Many mountain bikers appreciate the varied terrain, from olive groves to mountainous areas, and the options available for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or natural monuments to see along the mountain bike trails in Xert?
A: Yes, you can encounter several interesting landmarks and natural monuments. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3951882" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Millennial Olive Tree of the Four Legs (Canet lo Roig)&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/329152" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mola de Xert&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1160578" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serolla Stone Well&lt;/a&gt;. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3865075" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chapel and Fountain of Sant Vicent&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of facilities or attractions are available near the mountain bike trails in Xert?
A: Beyond the trails, you can find places like the historic village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5527451" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vallibona&lt;/a&gt;. For those looking for accommodation or services, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6416466" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciudad del Ciclismo Campsite&lt;/a&gt; is also nearby. Other points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5620781" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Royal Sanctuary of Our Lady of the Fountain of Health of Traiguera&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/662867" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cervera del Maestre Castle Ruins&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any moderate mountain bike routes in Xert?
A: Absolutely. Xert has a good selection of moderate trails, with over 50 routes in this category. A good example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38217216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Mas d'en Coll – Font de l'Aguilona loop from Sant Mateu&lt;/a&gt;, a 16.1 miles (25.9 km) route that offers a balanced challenge.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Xert?
A: While specific seasonal recommendations aren't provided, the varied terrain of Xert, with its olive groves and mountainous areas, suggests that spring and autumn would likely offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking. Summers might be hot, and winters could be cooler, especially at higher elevations.

Q: Can I find trails with significant elevation gain in Xert?
A: Yes, if you're looking for elevation gain, Xert's mountainous terrain delivers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931110078/vallibona-and-el-turmell-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vallibona and El Turmell Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is a difficult 28.7 miles (46.2 km) path that features over 1300 meters of ascent, providing panoramic views.
tips:
It is a thousand-year-old specimen within the Canet lo Roig estate that has the largest number of the entire Valencian Community (1131 specimens).
Some of the olive trees were studied by the Polytechnic University of Madrid, which determined their date of planting in the year 569 AD.
Among so many ancient olive trees, there is one that stands out and it is the one nicknamed "4 legs" that has even won an award for its shape and beauty and that is also related to war stories serving as a refuge.
Definitely a place to visit with the whole family!

waypoints.name: Millennial Olive Tree of the Four Legs (Canet lo Roig)
